items.title: Israel's defence network challenged by Iranian cluster ballistic missiles
items.summary: As the regional conflict between Israel and Iran enters a volatile new phase, a banned weapon of war is reappearing over civilian centres. While Israel’s defence systems remain robust, a new threat - the cluster ballistic missile - is testing the limits of safety. Unlike a single warhead, these missiles release hundreds of "bomblets," turning neighbourhoods into active minefields long after the sirens stop.

items.title: 'Typosquatting': How to spot fake news sites created by AI
items.summary: Courrier France 24, Sud Ouest Direct, 20minutes.com… they sound like real news outlets, but they are not. Learn how to spot sites created by AI.

items.title: India's historic haveli homes caught between revival and ruin
items.summary: Once the grand residences of Mughal-era nobility, the Indian capital's haveli homes now stand at a fragile crossroads – a handful lovingly restored but many more sliding quietly into ruin. Across Old Delhi cracked facades, shuttered gateways and sagging balconies tell the story of a heritage under siege.

items.title: Mohamed "Mo" Salah to leave Liverpool at the end of season
items.summary: ⚽ Mohamed "Mo" #Salah will leave #Liverpool at the end of the season in June, the club announced on Tuesday. He spent nine years at #Anfield, during which he established himself as one of the greatest players in the club’s history.

items.title: NATO officer says allies must urgently rethink defence, prepare for 'new enemy'
items.summary: A top NATO official on Wednesday urged allied countries to rethink how they ensure their defence. Pierre Vandier told a security and defence forum in Paris that many countries have yet to fully grasp the scale of the challenge, particularly when it comes to boosting weapons production, adding that it was not a question of money but of speed.

items.title: Where do French parties stand a year before the presidential election?
items.summary: With local elections done and dusted, France is pivoting towards electing a president to replace Emmanuel Macron in 2027. The local elections have revealed that the political landscape is fragmented, and that party alliances have reshaped. On the left, the socialist party is in turmoil over an alliance with the radical left party France Unbowed. On the right, the question is whether to move toward the centre of further shift right, towards the far-right party National Rally. FRANCE 24's Clovis C

items.title: Ex-EU border chief faces French investigation for crimes against humanity
items.summary: The Paris Court of Appeal opened an investigation into the former head of the EU Border agency on March 24 for potentially participating in crimes against humanity. The former border chief, Fabrice Leggeri resigned in 2022 following allegations of mistreatment of migrants at EU borders. Now a lawmaker for France's far-right National Rally party, he denies the allegations.

items.title: Denmark faces tough coalition talks following election
items.summary: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen handed in her government's resignation. The move comes after her three-party coalition suffered a large defeat in March 25's snap elections. Frederiksen faces tough coalition talks if she wants to remain PM. Since neither the left nor right leaning blocs won a majority in Denmark, Frederiksen's fate likely lies is in the hands of the centrist Moderate party of Lars Lugge Rasmussen, a foreign minister in her own government.

items.title: Heritage sites in the Middle East under threat due to conflict
items.summary: The Middle East war is having an impact on the region's heritage sites. Archaeological and Unesco heritage sites have been collateral damage in the war, on all sides. Unesco believes at least 6 heritage sites in the Middle East have been damaged, the Tyre site in Lebanon, four in Iran and the White City in Israel. In Iran, the historic city of isfahan and the Golestan palace in Tehran were also damaged.

items.title: Iran 'dismisses' US 15-point peace plan, issues its own ceasefire proposal
items.summary: Iran on March 25 an American plan to pause the war in the Middle East, issuing its own counterproposal instead, as it launched more attacks on Israel and Gulf Arab countries. Its own plan includes a halt to killings of its officials, means to make sure no other war is waged against it, reparations for the war, the end of hostilities and Iran’s “exercise of sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.” FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look.

items.title: Tout comprendre au cadmium, ce métal toxique qui contamine l'alimentation des Français
items.summary: Un Français sur deux possède des taux de cadmium trop importants dans son organisme, alerte l'Agence nationale de sécurité sanitaire mercredi. La cause de cette contamination ? Très largement le contenu de leur assiette. Origine, aliments concernés et nombreux risques pour la santé… France 24 fait le point sur ce qu'il faut savoir sur ce métal lourd.

translation.title: To understand everything with cadmium, this toxic metal that contaminates the diet of the French
translation.summary: One Frenchman in two has too high levels of cadmium in his body, warns the National Health Security Agency on Wednesday. The cause of this contamination? Very widely the content of their plates. Origin, food concerned and many health risks... France 24 takes stock of what we need to know about this heavy metal.

items.title: Une guerre avec la Russie n'est pas inéluctable, affirme le chef d'état-major de l'armée de Terre
items.summary: Dans un entretien accordé à France 24, le général Pierre Schill, chef d'état-major de l'armée de Terre, réfute le caractère inéluctable d'une guerre avec la Russie, tout en confirmant un renforcement de la présence française au Proche-Orient. Concernant le service national volontaire, il dit avoir confiance en la volonté des jeunes de s'engager pour la France.

translation.title: A war with Russia is not inevitable, says the Chief of Staff of the Army of Earth
translation.summary: In an interview given to France 24, General Pierre Schill, Chief of Staff of the Army of Earth, refutes the inevitable nature of a war with Russia, while confirming a strengthening of the French presence in the Middle East. Concerning the voluntary national service, he said he had confidence in the willingness of young people to commit to France.

items.title: Sénégal : des pêcheurs locaux en lutte face à la pêche industrielle et illégale
items.summary: Au large des côtes sénégalaises, les poissons disparaissent. Dans la ville portuaire de Rufisque, les pêcheurs qui travaillent dans ces eaux depuis des décennies affirment que les flottes industrielles et illégales ont complètement vidé la mer.

translation.title: Senegal: Local fishermen fighting against industrial and illegal fishing
translation.summary: Off the coast of Senegal, fish disappear. In the port city of Rufisque, fishermen who have been working in these waters for decades claim that industrial and illegal fleets have completely emptied the sea.

items.title: Viktor Orban annonce que la Hongrie va arrêter "progressivement les livraisons de gaz" à l'Ukraine
items.summary: Le Premier ministre hongrois, Viktor Orban, a annoncé mercredi qu'il comptait "réduire progressivement les livraisons de gaz" à l'Ukraine, tant qu'elle n'aura pas rétabli l'approvisionnement du pays en pétrole russe via l'oléoduc Droujba.

translation.title: Viktor Orban announces that Hungary will stop "progressively gas deliveries" to Ukraine
translation.summary: The Hungarian Prime Minister, Viktor Orban, announced on Wednesday that he intends to "gradually reduce gas deliveries" to Ukraine until it restores the country's supply of Russian oil via the Droujba pipeline.

items.title: Guerre au Soudan : le Tchad a fermé sa frontière
items.summary: Tout au long de la semaine dernière, des affrontements entre l’Armée soudanaise et les rebelles des FSR ont eu lieu dans l’ouest du Darfour à la frontière avec le Tchad. Des affrontements qui ont plusieurs fois débordé sur le territoire tchadien. Le Tchad a mobilisé plusieurs centaines de soldats à sa frontière avec le Soudan pour empêcher toute intrusion des belligérants. Explications de notre journaliste Harold Girard.

translation.title: War in Sudan: Chad closed its border
translation.summary: Throughout last week, clashes between the Sudanese Army and the SRF rebels took place in western Darfur on the border with Chad. Confrontations that have repeatedly overflowed Chadian territory. Chad mobilized several hundred soldiers on its border with the Sudan to prevent any intrusion by the belligerents. Explain our journalist Harold Girard.

items.title: La RD Congo va engager son deuxième recensement, le premier depuis 1984
items.summary: La RD Congo s’engage à faire son deuxième recensement général de la population. Cela fait plus de 40 ans que la population n’a pas été recensé dans le pays. Pour l’occasion, les autorités congolaises ont tenu une table ronde avec les bailleurs de fonds qui soutiennent ce projet. Une annonce qui relance le débat de l’organisation des élections générales prévues en 2028. Correspondance d'Aurélie Bazzara Kibangula.

translation.title: DR Congo to start its second census, the first since 1984
translation.summary: DR Congo is committed to conducting its second general population census. The population has not been recorded in the country for more than 40 years. For the occasion, the Congolese authorities held a round table with the donors supporting the project. An announcement that revives the debate on the organisation of the general elections scheduled for 2028. Correspondence of Aurélie Bazzara Kibangula.

items.title: Proche de Cuba, le pétrolier russe Anatoly Kolodkin brouille les pistes et provoque les États-Unis
items.summary: Le pétrolier russe Anatoly Kolodkin se dirige vers les Caraïbes et potentiellement Cuba, où il pourrait livrer des centaines de milliers de barils de brut. Une précieuse cargaison pour l'île privée de pétrole par l'administration Trump. Washington se passerait volontiers d’un face-à-face avec un navire russe dans sa sphère d’influence.

translation.title: Close to Cuba, the Russian tanker Anatoly Kolodkin blurs the tracks and provokes the United States
translation.summary: The Russian tanker Anatoly Kolodkin is heading for the Caribbean and potentially Cuba, where it could deliver hundreds of thousands of barrels of crude oil. A precious cargo for the private island of oil by the Trump administration. Washington would gladly do without a face-to-face with a Russian ship in its sphere of influence.

items.title: Avant France - Brésil, retour sur quatre confrontations légendaires entre les Bleus et la Seleçao
items.summary: À l'approche du match amical entre la France et le Brésil jeudi, l'excitation monte autour de ce duel de gala. Si Didier Deschamps et Kylian Mbappé saluent la grandeur de la Seleçao, ce match amical s'inscrit surtout dans la légende d'une rivalité historique. De l'éclosion du roi Pelé en 1958 aux chefs-d'œuvre de Zinedine Zidane, retour sur quatre rencontres qui ont forgé le mythe entre les Bleus et les Auriverdes.

translation.title: Before France - Brazil, back on four legendary confrontations between the Blues and the Seleçao
translation.summary: As the friendly match between France and Brazil approaches Thursday, the excitement rises around this duel of gala. While Didier Deschamps and Kylian Mbappé salute the greatness of the Seleçao, this friendly match is part of the legend of historical rivalry. From the outbreak of King Pelé in 1958 to the masterpieces of Zinedine Zidane, back on four encounters that forged the myth between the Blues and the Auriverdes.

items.title: Un drone ukrainien heurte une centrale électrique en Estonie, un autre s'écrase en Lettonie
items.summary: Deux drones militaires ukrainiens, partis ⁠pour frapper des ​cibles russe, ont pénétré dans l'espace aérien de l'Estonie et de la Lettonie, le premier heurtant ​la cheminée d'une centrale électrique tandis que le second a dû effectuer un atterrissage forcé, ont annoncé les deux pays baltes. Récit d'Étienne Paponaud.

translation.title: Ukrainian drone hit one power plant in Estonia, another crashes in Latvia
translation.summary: Two Ukrainian military drones, set off to hit Russian targets, entered the airspace of Estonia and Latvia, the first colliding with the chimney of an electric power plant while the second had to make a forced landing, the two Baltic countries announced. Story by Étienne Paponaud.

translation.title: Circular on the conduct of the Fourth National Agricultural Census issued by the State Council
translation.summary: Xinhua News, Beijing, 5 June In accordance with the Statistical Law of the People ' s Republic of China and the Regulations on the National Agricultural Census, the State Council decided to conduct the Fourth National Agricultural Census in 2026. The circular stressed that the Fourth National Agricultural Census was a major national survey on the new quest for a comprehensive revival of the Chinese nation based on China's modernization. It was guided by the socialist philosophy of Chinese identity in the new era of Xi Jinping. It was to follow up on the 20th and 20th China-China Congresses and the 3rd China-China Congresses of the Party's 20th and 20th China-China Congresses, and to ensure that the Party's central and State Councils' decisions were deployed in good faith. It was determined by law to conduct a census, a scientific survey and a census of the people, to be realistic and innovative and to carry out a comprehensive mapping of the country's “three farmers” in the new era, which objectively reflected the new state of agricultural development, new aspects of rural construction, new changes in rural life and new effects of rural reform. The Circular states that the Fourth National Agricultural Census is aimed at rural households in the People ' s Republic of China, including rural agricultural producers and other households; urban agricultural producers; agricultural production operators; village committees; and town and town people ' s governments. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time for the census was 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period was annual 2026. items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 中央军委主席习近平日前签署命令，发布《军事设施建设条例》，自2025年8月1日起施行。 条例深入贯彻习近平强军思想，深入贯彻新时代军事战略方针，聚焦服务备战打仗，适应新的领导指挥体制和保障模式，科学规范军事设施建设的基本原则、管理体制、运行机制和工作制度，是军事设施建设工作的重要依据。 条例共11章63条，按照军委管总、战区主战、军种主建的总原则，围绕理顺建设链、管理链、监督链，重点从军事设施建设计划管理、项目审批、工程发包、勘察设计、施工管理、应急建设、安全保密、监督问责等方面，厘清管理权限，优化流程机制，固化实践成果，为提高军事设施建设质量效益和保障能力提供有力法治保障。

items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee ' s Law Enforcement Inspection Unit on Energy Conservation Law that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection was carried out through a combination of on-site and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others, and that the Standing Committee of the People ' s Congress of Beijing, Shanxi, Jilin, Chongqing and others was entrusted with the task of examining the implementation of the law on energy conservation in the administrative region. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, model studies, thematic studies, spot checks and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with legislation; other issues in law enforcement; and suggestions and recommendations for amendments to energy-saving laws.

items.summary: Meta is preparing for its largest-ever round of layoffs, with employees in wearables and ads told to work from home as cuts loom. Reuters reported the reductions could affect 20% of its nearly 79,000-strong workforce—around 15,800 jobs. The move is driven by Meta's $600 billion AI infrastructure commitment and a broader push toward leaner, AI-assisted operations, compounded by setbacks with its in-house AI models.

items.title: 'Cricket is my passion. No one can take this away from me': Prithvi Shaw
items.summary: Prithvi Shaw opens up on his year-long reset after missing IPL 2025, calling it a much-needed mental break. The Delhi Capitals opener reflects on inconsistent form, handling criticism, and lessons from setbacks, including his Mumbai exit. Back with a clearer mindset, Shaw emphasises quality practice, staying at present, and his unwavering ambition to return to the Indian team.

items.title: Hormuz is a trailer. Malacca is China’s real nightmare — and India knows it
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz crisis highlights global maritime vulnerabilities. For China, the Strait of Malacca presents a similar, long-term strategic risk. India's Andaman and Nicobar Islands offer proximity and surveillance capabilities, providing significant leverage. This geographical advantage allows India to create uncertainty and pressure, impacting China's vital trade and energy lifelines.

items.title: West Bengal polls: RG Kar victim’s mother Ratna Debnath gets BJP ticket from Panihati
items.summary: The BJP has nominated Ratna Debnath, mother of the RG Kar Medical College rape and murder victim, from Panihati for the West Bengal Assembly elections. Her decision to contest on a BJP ticket stems from her pursuit of justice for her daughter and a broader fight for women's safety. This move has sparked debate among activists.

items.title: H-1B registration dips 30-50%: Companies that are emerging as ‘favourites’ in new system
items.summary: H-1B visa registrations for FY27 have plummeted by 30-50%, driven by increased fees and a new wage-based lottery. This shift favors mid-tier IT firms and U.S. Master's degree holders, while large outsourcing companies face reduced dominance. The system now prioritizes higher wages, signaling a move towards quality over sheer volume in talent acquisition.

items.title: Jannik Sinner scripts history, breaks Djokovic's 10-year-old record
items.summary: Jannik Sinner reached the Miami Open quarter-finals after beating Alex Michelsen and setting a record of 28 consecutive Masters 1000 sets, surpassing Novak Djokovic. Despite a tough fight, he won in straight sets. Sinner stays on track for the Sunshine Double. Elsewhere, Frances Tiafoe advanced, while Martin Landaluce impressed with an emotional upset win.
